rationale: The page highlights two significant architectural structures from the era of Asaf-ud-Daula: the Bara Imambara and the Rumi Darwaza, providing brief descriptions of their unique features and architectural inspirations.
cleanedText:
Architectural Splendour of Asaf-ud-Daula's Era
Bara Imambara
A magnificent monument featuring one of the largest arched halls without support beams
Rumi Darwaza
An imposing gateway inspired by Istanbul's architecture, symbolising Lucknow's grandeur
